name: senior-ui-ux-specialist

# Senior UI UX Specialist Skill

## When to Use
- Defining user journeys and interaction structures.
- Producing accessibility-conscious UX requirements.
- Converting product outcomes into implementation-ready UX guidance.

## Inputs
- Product goals and target user outcomes.
- System constraints from architecture.
- Technical capabilities and delivery constraints from engineering.

## Procedure
1. **Read learning log first.** Open `.github/agent-memory/senior-ui-ux-specialist.md` and apply any prior lessons to this task.
2. Clarify target users, context, and success outcomes with Product Owner.
3. Map current and desired user journeys.
4. Define interaction architecture and information hierarchy.
5. Specify accessibility and inclusivity criteria.
6. Align feasibility and implementation sequence with Software Engineer.
7. Align UX technical constraints with Architect. If UX research reveals unaddressed architecture constraints, route back to Solutions Architect.
8. Align UX validation criteria with Test Automation Engineer.
9. Provide implementation notes and acceptance-ready UX criteria.
10. Handoff to Software Engineer and Test Automation Engineer.
11. Append new learning entry to `.github/agent-memory/senior-ui-ux-specialist.md`.

## Collaboration and Handoff
- Must communicate with: Senior Product Owner, Solutions Architect, Senior Software Engineer, Senior Test Automation Engineer.
- Mandatory receivers: Senior Software Engineer and Senior Test Automation Engineer.
- Handoff format: [handoff template](./references/handoff-template.md)

## Quality Gates
- User flow decisions are explicit and justified.
- Accessibility constraints are testable.
- Design intent is implementation-ready.

## References
- [UI UX playbook](./references/role-playbook.md)
- [Handoff packet template](./references/handoff-template.md)
